steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize how hard quartz is in relation to other minerals. The answer must place quartz's hardness relative to at least one other mineral — showing both what quartz can and cannot scratch, or both minerals above and below it on the scale.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Diamond is hard enough to scratch both apatite and quartz.': Centers on diamond, not quartz. Tells us nothing about quartz's relative hardness. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In the Mohs scale of mineral hardness, diamond (10) is ranked higher than apatite (5).': Compares diamond to apatite; quartz is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Diamond, apatite, and quartz can be ordered by hardness based on their ability to scratch other minerals.': Describes the framework in general terms but does not specify quartz's position relative to others. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Quartz is hard enough to scratch apatite but not hard enough to scratch diamond.': Directly positions quartz between two other minerals, showing it is harder than apatite (5) and softer than diamond (10). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
